evidence.title: #bars #france #belgium #drinks #alcohol | Philippe Gijsels | 38 comments
evidence.description: I just love this chart. Drinking one glas in every one of them would take you a while… I wonder about Belgium. Totally red I would assume #bars #France #Belgium #drinks #Alcohol | 38 comments on LinkedIn
evidence.text:
I just love this chart. Drinking one glas in every one of them would take you a while… I wonder about Belgium. Totally red I would assume #bars #France #Belgium #drinks #Alcohol
What shall we do with the drunken sailor? Philippe Gijsels Koen De Leus cind du bois Erik Op de Beeck Lara Van den Heuvel Jacobine van der Kun Eric Raets, CFA Dominique Bastin Klaus Sauer Bernd Mosig and all https://youtu.be/qGyPuey-1Jw
It'd be interesting to see that paired with population data so as to have a glimpse on a "bars per capita" metric.
We lost 30% of our bars in 10 years time that’s why we started our Kroegtijgers initiative in Belgium and soon in Netherlands 🙏
One of the more amusing and surprisingly useful French-made mobile apps I discovered while living in France was MrGoodBeer, which shows you the bars around you and the price of a pint at each on a map 🇫🇷🍺📍This was back in 2015, but it looks like it's still around (and not sure if it retains the same basic functionality it had >10 years ago) https://www.mistergoodbeer.com/en
Sorry to kill your assumption but at the going rate , bars closing definitively due to “hate” & “destroy” legislation vs horeca , always instigated by the same people and party , soon the map of Belgium will be BLANK …
What would happen in a Belgium vs Wisconsin match-up? I think we would have fun finding out.
Everybody needs a hobby.
Something is off with this data... the entirety of rural Bretagne has a higher concentration than many big cities?? Still, it looks like a fun challenge
This is why I was so happy when I lived there!!!

evidence.page_title: All The Bars In France That Primarily Serve Alcohol - Brilliant Maps
evidence.title: All The Bars In France That Primarily Serve Alcohol - Brilliant Maps
evidence.description: Map created by reddit user T9C-gars
evidence.text:
The map above shows the 3,209 bars which primarily serve alcohol in mainland France. This compares to 34,669 “mixed establishments”, such as restaurants, night clubs, etc. which also sell alcohol across France.
The data comes from Frace’s INSEE or National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies.
A few interesting things pop-out about bars:
- Bretons (north-west France) clearly like them.
- Skiers in the Alps also clearly like them.
- Bars are popular along France’s borders with most other countries, except Spain.
- Bars can be commonly found along most of the coast of France.
It should also be noted that Paris looks like it has fewer bars than it really does, due to the way they are being displayed.
Want to find the right bar? The following guides may be of some help:
- France: 100 Locals Tell You Where to Go, What to Eat, & How to Fit In
- Eating & Drinking in Paris, 6th Edition (Eating and Drinking in Paris)
- MICHELIN Guide France 2015: Hotels & Restaurants (Michelin Guides) (French Edition)
Like this map? Please share it with a friend:
image_captions: All The Bars In France Which Primarily Serve Alcohol

Alex Sewell liked thisAlex Sewell liked thisInexperienced leaders want others to be "more coachable." But are they good coaches? Being coachable requires a growth mindset, intrinsic motivation, and openness to reason. These traits are deep and define how a person interacts with reality. A person who considers performance to be about tolerating dysfunction, placating insecure egos, and being the most likeable is unlikely to be driven to do their life's work. These two attitudes (political survival vs. excellence) rarely coexist in one person. There's nuance here: some level of dealing with an imperfect environment is necessary, and holding oneself to the same standard one expects from others matters. Good coaching requires three things: invoking a relevant guideline, applying it correctly to the given situation, and explaining it clearly so people can understand and apply it. Here's how it goes wrong. Exhorting people to be more careful or work harder where fixing fragile systems and error-prone patterns would be a more proper, systemic response. Public shaming is bad, but the right application is calling out the problem, not the person, with tact and finesse. The wrong application is keeping a team pretending not to see glaring issues, in fear of offending others. "Communicating more clearly" is everyone's favorite vague feedback. A much more effective way is modeling clear communication in the first place and showing what better looks like. People "don't listen" because the message is either incoherent, leading to double binds like being "too slow" or making "too many mistakes," unintelligible like "I'll know it when I see it," or too abstract given their more junior perspective. Leaders like to say: if you can't explain it simply and concisely, you don't really understand it. Time to apply this to giving feedback.

Alex Sewell reacted on thisAlex Sewell reacted on thisI was looking at this data from the McKinsey survey and it highlights a significant trend in the healthcare industry: while chatbots are often the focus, the real transformation is occurring with multiagent workflows. Here’s a breakdown of the current landscape: - Gen AI is advancing rapidly, with 50% of healthcare leaders already implementing it. We are transitioning from the "experimentation" phase to actual production. - The adoption of multiagent systems is on the rise, with nearly 20% having deployed multiagent workflows and 51% currently in the proof-of-concept stage. - Technology providers are at the forefront, as 64% of healthcare technology and service firms have already integrated Gen AI. Why is this important? Multiagent workflows signify a shift from "AI that talks to you" to "AI that works for you." Instead of relying on a single model, multiple agents collaborate to tackle complex tasks such as coordinating patient care and automating administrative billing processes. This evolution represents the difference between having a digital assistant and a digital team. The shift from "we're thinking about it" to "we're doing it" is occurring more rapidly than anticipated. If you haven't started exploring how these agents interact, you may find yourself behind the curve by next year. Have you begun transitioning to multiagent systems, or are you still focused on refining single-use Gen AI? #HealthcareAI #DigitalTransformation #HealthTech #GenAI #Uk #Europe

Pieter Waasdorp
9K followers
Nearly every Belgian household has internet access, but what happens when devices reach the end of their life? Surprisingly, Belgian consumers prefer keeping old smartphones and laptops in drawers rather than selling them. A new analysis of Belgian buying and recycling habits reveals that price and design dominate when purchasing new devices. Two out of three Belgians, 66.4%, choose price as their main criterion, while sustainability ranks far lower compared to other EU countries. When devices such as Apple iPhones, tablets, or laptops are no longer in use, most Belgians do not resell them. Only 12.4% of mobile phones are sold second-hand, the lowest rate in Europe. In contrast, Belgium performs better on recycling, ranking second in the European Union for recycling desktops, laptops, tablets, and smartphones. Experts argue that resale plays a critical role in extending device lifecycles and reducing the demand for new raw materials. More focus on resale could bring Belgium in line with European leaders like the Netherlands and Germany, while also supporting a more circular economy. Read the full article on Secondarymarket.news to discover the complete figures and trends shaping Belgian consumer behaviour. #Smartphones #Refurbished #CircularEconomy #Belgium #SecondaryMarket

Thank you Trends Kanaal Z for covering the growing importance of circularity in ICT - and for mentioning / quoting us! We at Circular IT group see on a daily basis that there is a growing need for access to reuse of pre-owned ICT devices, especially in today's turbulent market conditions! We particularly see that: 1. Companies look for sustainable solutions for their used ICT devices (to monitize the residual value and/or donate to charities like Close the Gap or Digital for Youth), to find an alternative for the traditional downstream ♻️ 2. Companies (large & small) look for high quality (and readily available!) pre-owned ICT devices with warranty, because of the better price/quality (lower cost compared to new with same quality & warranty) and because of the fast availability (up to same-day delivery!) 🕙 3. Families & individuals also find their way more & more towards pre-owned devices, because they want to have a positive impact and because they want to cut costs in today ever-more-expensive world. 💲 Do you want more info on how you can get access to high quality used ICT devices? Do check our webshops www.refurbisheddirect.com or www.iUsed.com (or contact anyone from the Circular IT group) PS: Thank you Shima & Leen for showing that circularity in ICT can be achieved with a big smile 🙂

Today is Anti-Ransomware Day. A moment to pause and reflect on one of the most persistent and damaging cyber threats facing organizations all over the world. Our Dark Web monitoring reveals that in 2024 alone, data from at least 45 Belgian companies has been published on leak sites. The vast majority of these victims are SMEs. At least 1.35 terabytes of data was stolen, and these numbers only reflect the known cases. The real impact is likely much greater. We released these findings today to raise awareness of the growing risk of ransomware attacks. A sincere thank you to Data News, Het Laatste Nieuws, Solutions Magazine and other media for publishing these stats and helping raise awareness. Because awareness is the first step toward resilience. Also a special thanks to Simen V., our Strategic Advisor, for leading this research and analysis. Soo… how do you build and grow manufacturing software internationally when:

 - Europe’s (automotive) industry is under unprecedented pressure
 - The geopolitical climate is through the roof
 - Cybersecurity threats are looming over production companies
 - And then there’s AI AI AI.everywhere. Huge promises… but will it truly live up its expectations?

It can feel like planning in a minefield.

Together with entrepreneurs - Aline Muylaert, Zhong Xu, koen dejonckheere, Pieterjan Desmet, Chris Van de Voorde, and Jürgen Ingels - we shed some light in Trends on this complex reality and how to navigate it.

No silver bullets. No easy answers.
Just a shared sense of entrepreneurial realism.

Happy reading!

Thank you Stijn Fockedey & Laurens Bouckaert 🙏
